title: Debugger Preferences
html:
Debugger Preferences

Add to My Topics

Email LinkCopy Link

    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Specify general preferences for the Debugger and save and load state settings in the configuration file.

Preferences control or specify:

  • Whether the Debugger’s active state should be loaded and applied at Debugger startup (from the last-used state or the default state)
  • Whether the active state should be saved (as the last-used state) at Debugger shutdown
  • The Debugger form windows to be opened at startup
  • The Open Structure form should do an automatic retrieve on startup
  • Individually tailored, named states that can be saved for use in later Debugger sessions

When you start the Debugger for the first time, the Debugger attempts to load a configuration file (udbg.cnf). If it finds no preferences, it establishes a default state to be used for the active Debugger session. This default state is saved only if On close: save stateis selected, or if you click Save.

Two unnamed states are always written to the configuration file on save—the last-used state and the default state.

Debugger Preferences

Understand the various preferences that can be set for the Debugger.

On startup: apply default state
Apply the default state from the configuration file to the current active state on Debugger startup.
On startup: apply last state
Apply the default state from the configuration file to the current active state on Debugger startup.
On close: save state
Save the Debugger’s preferences and internal list of states to the configuration file when the Debugger is closed.
On startup: open Browser
Open the Browser window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Quick Watch
Open the Quick Watch window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Settings
Open the Settings window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Error Context
Open the Error Context window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Message Frame
Open the Message Frame window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Call Stack
Open the Call Stack window when the Debugger is started.
On startup: open Preferences
Open the Debugger Preferences window when the Debugger is started.
Automatic retrieve for Open Structure
Automatically retrieve data when starting the Open Structure window. Selected by default.
State of Breakpoint, Settings, and Watches stored as:
Apply the default state, last-used state, or specify and apply named states that you can individually tailor for your specific debugging needs.
  • Add—add a new named state to the Debugger’s internal list of states, using the name and description you enter. Click Save to insert a new section with this name and description, containing the current active state, in the configuration file.
  • Remove—remove the currently selected named state from the Debugger’s internal list of states.
  • Update—updates the selected named state in the Debugger’s internal list of states, using the current active state.
  • Update Default—updates the default state using the current active state. This is handy for establishing a minimum set of preferences settings, breakpoints, and watches, that you can use for any debugging session.
  • Apply—changes the current active state using the selected named state.
  • Apply Default—changes the current active state using the default state in the Debugger ’s internal list of states.

Debugger Buttons

Understand the buttons that might display for the Debugger.

  • Close—close the Preferences form, keeping your changes to preferences and Debugger state, but not storing them permanently.
  • Save—save the current preferences and Debugger state to the configuration file, including any changes you made to the preferences, the active state, the default state, and named states.
  • Load—load the configuration file.
Related Links
  • Debugger Settings

    Specify diagnostic settings for the current Debugger session.
